intro: Licensed Professional Counselor. Husband. Father of three. Founder of Morning Light Counseling.
bio:
I'm Billy — a Licensed Professional Counselor in Pennsylvania, where I also provide clinical supervision for pre-licensed counselors coming up in the field.

Before Morning Light, my work in counseling and organizational leadership kept surfacing the same gap: men were quietly struggling, and almost nothing was built for them. Men will fix a truck, a deck, or a spreadsheet before they'll say a word about what's actually wearing them down. I started this practice because that silence has a cost — and because I believe change is possible when a man finally has a place to do the work.

Outside the office, I'm a husband and a dad to three kids, which means most of what I know about patience has been field-tested.
approachBody:
My approach blends narrative work with a Biblical framework. In plain terms: every man is living out a story — about who he is, what he's worth, and what he's supposed to carry. Some of those stories are true. Some of them were handed to him a long time ago and have been quietly running the show ever since.

In counseling we slow down enough to look at that story honestly, hold it up against what God says is true, and start writing the next chapter differently. It's not quick, and it's not passive — but it's real, and it lasts.
credentials: Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC), Commonwealth of Pennsylvania
Focused practice: counseling for men, ages 12 and up
quote: If you want to build a ship, don't drum up the men to gather wood, divide the work, and give orders. Instead, teach them to yearn for the vast and endless sea.
